🎯 Exit Strategy — Live Levels (re-derived today)
VERA
✅ HOLD — 8 DAYS TO PDUFA
Stop: ~$36 — re-derived below $38 pre-run base. Stored $24.50 is stale at $43 entry (director buy zone from $20–22 irrelevant now). At $43 entry the risk-relevant floor is the pre-breakout consolidation base ~$38 minus 1–3% buffer = ~$36. This stop only fires on an unexpected pre-PDUFA event (clinical hold, material disclosure) — binary events can gap -60% on PDUFA date regardless of stop placement.
TP1: $55 — post-approval stabilization. IgAN analogs (TERN, BAIP) settled +40–50% post-approval. Sell 50% here.
TP2: $78 — 14-analyst consensus midpoint ($68–$83). Sell remaining 50%.
Binary strategy: hold through July 7. No action pre-PDUFA.
Approval (89% base rate): Gap to $55+ on open. Trim 50% at TP1, hold 3 shares for TP2 $78. Rejection: Expect $12–17 (−60–70%). Stop won't help on gap-down. Decision made at open July 7.
ANET
✅ HOLD — 26 DAYS TO EXIT✅ HALAL VERIFIED (Musaffa, May 26 2026 — 0% debt, 4.14% interest income)
Stop: $144 ✅ CONFIRMED — sits between $136.70 and $146.94 support zones. Fires after $146.94 breaks and confirms, not on a test. Distance from $157.60 = -8.6%. Levels: 200-day MA $131.20, 50-day MA $128.50 (both well below current — healthy).
TP1 (revised): $179.80 — ATH April 24, 2026. Real resistance. Stored $185 is above ATH — achievable only on ATH breakout. Distance from $157.60 = +14.1%. Plan: trim 50–60% at ATH reclaim, then hold rest to $185 if breakout holds.
TP2: $185 / Full exit by July 25 — analyst cluster avg $190.09. Hard deadline July 25 before Q2 earnings ~Aug 5 binary risk. Distance +17.4%.
Today: ANET -4.7% from Friday $165.45. Limit at $157 filled on continued macro rotation selling. Third down day in a row (Jun 23: -7.1%, Jun 26: -6.4%, Jun 29: -4.7%). Momentum is weak short-term. Watch $147 support — a close below would trigger stop review.
AI Ethernet narrative intact. Q1 revenue +35.1% YoY, Q2 guided $2.8B. No ANET-specific negative news — this is sector rotation. Hold to July 25 exit window or $179.80 TP, whichever comes first.

📚 Today's Concept — Day 11 of 30
Hyperscaler Capex — the engine behind ANET
Hyperscalers — Microsoft, Amazon AWS, Alphabet Google Cloud, Meta — build the data centers the world rents. Their combined annual capex is the single biggest demand driver for the entire AI infrastructure supply chain. When MSFT raises capex guidance, ANET, NVDA, and AVGO all move. 2026 combined hyperscaler capex runs ~$725B, up 77% from $410B in 2025. Every dollar of that flows somewhere — and roughly 15–20% hits the networking layer, which is exactly what ANET sells. You bought ANET at $157 because AI Ethernet is the boring, high-margin pick-and-shovel beneath the AI data center buildout. The thesis: as long as hyperscalers keep building, ANET keeps shipping Q1 revenue +35.1% YoY, Q2 guided $2.8B. If you ever see the phrase "AI capex digestion" trending on FinTwit — that's the warning sign. It means hyperscalers are slowing spend, and ANET de-rates with the whole chain. Note: Day 29 (Part 2 — the actual dollar flows) was taught before this foundation. This is the base concept to go with it.
Also today: Capex intensity — capex as % of revenue. Rising = investing in future growth. META's capex intensity went from 18% to 35%+ in two years; that's the AI arms race in one number. When ANET's customers' capex intensity is rising, ANET's revenue forecast is rising — real-time.
